response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. Their professional C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience (RIDE Capital was retail investment tooling in Node/TypeScript, not capital markets systems), and no SQL Server or Oracle exposure is evidenced. Their depth is in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, which are largely outside this role's stack.
strengths: Deep systems programming experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (DLang, async I/O) that shares concepts with performance-critical C++ work
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development, plus DevOps and testing tooling experience
Long, consistent career progression with fluent English and demonstrated communication skills (workshops, talks, courses)
concerns: Professional C++ experience is roughly 7 months (2014–2015), versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems background
